description_long: This is a tall, slender wooden clock tower with a rectangular base and a slightly wider upper section housing a large, round clock face. The clock face is white with black Roman numerals and two black hands, encased in a simple arched frame. Below the clock face, a small door is open, revealing a golden pendulum inside the midsection of the tower. The wood grain texture is visible throughout, giving the structure a warm, natural appearance. The top of the tower is flat and unadorned, with no spire or decorative roof elements. The overall design is functional and modest, reminiscent of traditional grandfather clocks but scaled up for public display.
description: A tall, wooden clock tower with a white Roman numeral clock face and an open door revealing a golden pendulum. The design is simple, with visible wood grain and a flat top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the clock face, open door, and visible pendulum inside the wooden tower.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the tower and the open door protruding from the midsection.
description_view_2: Rear-side view showing the plain wooden back and the side of the open door.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with the tower's profile and the open door visible.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is round and white, featuring black Roman numerals for each hour, with two black hands (hour and minute). The face is set within a simple arched wooden frame, with no additional decorative elements or markings.
